revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍泰長]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1068|death_date=1121|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Abe no Yasunaga | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Late Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Jiryaku|en|Jiryaku|qid=Q934365|lt=Jiryaku|1=Jiryaku}} 4 (1068) | death_date = {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|qid=Q1194464|lt=Hōan|1=Hōan}} 2 (1121) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Head of the Bureau of Divination (Onmyōryō)]] | allegiance = [[Emperor Horikawa]] → [[Emperor Toba]] | clan = [[Abe clan (Japan)|Abe clan]] | parents = Father: [[Abe no Ariyuki]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = [[Abe no Masafumi|Masafumi]], [[Abe no Yasuuji|Yasuuji]], [[Abe no Yasutomo|Yasutomo]], [[Abe no Yasutoshi|Yasutoshi]] | notable_activities = }} '''Abe no Yasunaga''' (安倍 泰長) was a [[Late Heian period]] {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|qid=Q2274163|lt=onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, [[Abe no Ariyuki]]. His {{ill|court Rank|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452076}} was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and he served as the [[Yin-Yang Bureau|Head of the Bureau of Divination (Onmyōryō)]]. == Career == Abe no Yasunaga began his court career in {{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|qid=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}} 6 (1092) when he was granted his first Rank{{ref|『師通記』寛治6th year 正月4日条|qq=}}. The following year, he attempted to concurrently serve as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, though this effort was unsuccessful{{ref|『長右記』寛治7th year 2月5日条|qq=}}. Yasunaga was promoted to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=従五位|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=従五位}} in {{ill|Jōtoku|en|Jōtoku|qid=Q1193901|lt=Jōtoku|1=Jōtoku}} 3 (1099){{ref|『本朝世紀』承徳3rd year 正月6日条|qq=}}, and to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=正五位|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=正五位}} in {{ill|Tennin|en|Tennin|qid=Q7700291|lt=Tennin|1=Tennin}} 1 (1108){{ref|『中右記』天仁元年11月20日条|qq=}}. During this period, he held positions such as {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Head of the Bureau of Court Music (Gagaku)|ja|雅楽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Court Music (Gagaku)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head of the Bureau of Court Music (Gagaku)|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=雅楽寮|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=雅楽寮|1=Gagaku-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8963315}}. In {{ill|Ten'ei|en|Ten'ei|qid=Q1193915|lt=Ten'ei|1=Ten'ei}} 2 (1111), he was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=従四位|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=従四位}}{{ref|『長秋記』天永2nd year 2月14日条|qq=}}, and by {{ill|Eikyū|en|Eikyū|qid=Q543201|lt=Eikyū|1=Eikyū}} 2 (1114), he was serving as the Head of the Bureau of Divination. He passed away in {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|qid=Q1194464|lt=Hōan|1=Hōan}} 2 (1121) at the age of 54{{ref|赤澤、2011th year |qq=}}. == Family == * Father: [[Abe no Ariyuki]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown ''' Son: [[Abe no Masafumi]] ''' Son: [[Abe no Yasuuji]]{{ref name="a"|"Abe Clan Genealogy" (『続群書類従』巻第170所収)|qq=}} ''' Son: [[Abe no Yasutomo]] (1110–1183) ''' Son: [[Abe no Yasutoshi]]{{ref name="a"|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist|qq=}} == References == * Akazawa Haruhiko, "Development of the Abe Clan in the Kamakura Period," in *Studies on Kamakura Period Officials and Onmyōji* (吉川弘文館, 2011) ISBN 978-4-642-02893-6 * Sekiguchi Chikara, "Abe no Yasunaga," in *Heian Period Historical Dictionary* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7 * Makino Kōzō (ed.), *Heian Name Dictionary, Vol. 1* (Izumi Shoin, 2007) ISBN 978-4-7576-0396-7
{{translated page|ja|安倍泰長|version=86385963|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:安倍泰長]]
